title: ilastik install and run for Syn Bot (Windows Version)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bqpo2","text":"Video instructions for installing ilastik, training an ilastik project, and using the ilastik project for simple SynBot run for Windows operating system.   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: If video quality is poor, try watching directly from Youtube at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ApOwcOFMUcg
0:00 - 1:30 Install ilastik by downloading it from ilastik.org/download and running the installer.
1:30 - 3:00 Generate training images by running SynBot on a set of images so that any preprocessing steps will be the same as a SynBot run.
3:00 - 4:00 Copy two-color training images to a training images folder
4:00 - 4:20 Make empty folder for holding red images and green images
4:20 - 4:32 Run extract_channels.ijm macro from the extra code folder on the SynBot GitHub repository
4:32 - 5:22 Select merged images folder, then red images folder, then green images folder
5:22 - 5:39 Open ilastik and start a new Pixel Classification project
5:39 - 6:01 Save the ilastik project file to a safe place where you can use it to threshold with SynBot
6:01 - 6:20 Click add new image and select the red training image (or images)
6:20 - 6:31 Click the "2. Feature Selection" drop down then "select features" and use Ctrl + A to select all features.
6:31 - 6:34 Click the "3. Training" drop down to advance
6:34 - 6:41 Zoom in image using Ctrl + +
6:41 - 7:05 Use the Label 1 to mark several examples of foreground puncta. Only a handful are needed to get started.
7:21 - 7:37 Click Live Update to see a rendering of the ilastik model that will update when new markings are added. Use Ctrl + - to zoom out and check how well this model is working.
7:37 - 7:49 Save the ilastik project. This saved model will be used by SynBot and you do not need to change anything in the steps 4 or 5 in the ilastik pipeline.
7:55 - 9:37 Repeat steps 8-17 for the green channel image
9:37 - 9:51 Open and run SynBot.ijm
9:51 - 10:14 Enter your analysis parameters, including the ilastik thresholding method.
10:14 - 10:29 Select ilastik.exe application location. On Windows this will typically be in a path like C:/Program Files/ilastik1.4.0/ilastik.exe
10:29 - 10:34 Select the ilastik project file for thresholding the red channel.
10:34 - 10:40 Select the ilastik project file for thresholding the green channel.

10:40 - 10:42 Enter the threshold of ilastik confidence to use for the analysis. ilastik will give a confidence score from 0 to 1 of how likely the given pixel is part of the foreground. A good threshold is something between 0.5 and 0.9.
10:42 - 12:14 View results
7:05 - 7:21 Click Label 2 and use this to label some examples of background regions.
creator: Justin T Savage
